paper_title: Pollen Monitoring Program (PMP) Data Collection and Analysis

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting pollen data using traps in the PMP network. The traps are designed to capture annual pollen deposition, and the data is used to study pollen transport and deposition patterns.

steps.description: The pollen traps used in the PMP network generally consist of a bucket or bottle large enough to contain the annual surplus in precipitation on a surface of usually 19.6 cm² (5 cm diameter opening) or similar.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up pollen traps with specific dimensions to collect annual pollen deposition.

steps.description: The collection of the trap content was generally carried out annually and any special circumstances potentially affecting the annual pollen deposition were noted and stored in the database.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen trap content annually and record special circumstances.

steps.description: Concentrating the content of the traps was carried out either using filter paper or centrifugation and decanting the supernatant.
steps.searchable_summary: Concentrate pollen samples using filter paper or centrifugation.

steps.description: Pollen quantity was assessed by adding Lycopodium spore tablets to each trap before processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Add Lycopodium spore tablets to assess pollen quantity.

steps.description: Pollen concentration was obtained from the ratios between pollen grains counted to Lycopodium spike counted and Lycopodium spike added.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pollen concentration using Lycopodium ratios.
